text.title: Trunk-Based CI Gates Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the trunk-based-ci-gates methodology. Each pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Pre-commit hooks MUST run on staged files only and complete in under 1 second. Running the full test suite in pre-commit is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Slow hooks train developers to use --no-verify and the gate dies. Pre-commit is for the cheap obvious stuff (lint, format, secret scan); deep correctness is CI's job.

rule.statement: CI MUST complete in under 10 minutes wall time. If it exceeds, parallelise + cache + move slow integration suites to nightly.
rule.rationale: CI slower than 10 minutes makes developers batch pushes; the rapid feedback loop that makes trunk-based development safe disappears.

rule.statement: Branch protection MUST require ALL CI jobs in the required-status list, at least 1 review, linear history, and restrict direct pushes to automation. After any CI change adding/renaming jobs, the required-status list MUST be re-verified via gh api.
rule.rationale: A new CI job that runs but is not in required-status list does not gate merges; PRs merge with a failing job and trunk breaks silently.

rule.statement: A PR that breaks main MUST be reverted by bot (git revert -m 1) within 15 minutes. Manual reverts and 'one more fix on top' are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Human reverts are slow and incomplete; bot reverts are instantaneous and deterministic. Fix-forward in a new PR keeps trunk green and produces a clean blame trail.

rule.statement: Pre-commit hooks MUST be configured strictly enough that --no-verify is never required. CI MUST fail commits that lack the hook signature where applicable.
rule.rationale: Routine --no-verify usage means the hook is too slow or too aggressive; tighten or relax until it stays out of the developer's way.

rule.statement: Pre-commit MUST include a secret-scan hook (detect-secrets, gitleaks). CI MUST include a second secret scan as defence-in-depth.
rule.rationale: Secrets committed once and pushed are leaked forever; pre-commit catches most cases, CI catches what slipped through.
